title: fix: play page description — less marketing, more hackerspace
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Poking at models
gist.blurb: We scrubbed the emoji-heavy marketing fluff from the Play page. Now it's just what it says on the tin: a playground for poking at models.
gist.summary: Simplified the Play page copy to remove marketing language and consolidated the underlying translation keys.
gist.impact: The Play page now features a cleaner, more direct description that better matches our anti-corporate hackerspace vibe.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee in a cozy hackerspace, literally 'poking at a model' (a glowing geometric shape or small robot) with a tiny stick. Represents the new 'playground for poking at models' copy.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot in a dimly lit hackerspace, playfully poking a glowing geometric AI model with a tiny stick. A chunky retro computer monitor glows softly in the background. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and nostalgic.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot in a dimly lit hackerspace, playfully poking a glowing geometric AI model with a tiny stick.
